articles.title: Durant’s Game 3 Injury Status Threatens Rockets’ Season
articles.original_title: Kevin Durant's Game 3 Injury Status Puts Rockets' Season in Serious Jeopardy - Sports Illustrated
articles.summary: Kevin Durant’s uncertain health ahead of Game 3 has raised concerns for the Houston Rockets’ playoff hopes. The injury report suggests he may miss the game, which could significantly diminish the Rockets’ chances of advancing.
key_points: Durant’s status for Game 3 is listed as questionable or out.
The Rockets rely heavily on Durant’s scoring and leadership in the series.
Missing Durant could tilt the series in the Lakers’ favor.

articles.title: Capela ‘Surprised’ by Ayton Comments Ahead of Lakers‑Rockets Series
articles.original_title: Lakers News: Clint Capela ‘Surprised’ By Deandre Ayton Comments
articles.summary: Clint Capela expressed surprise at remarks made by Deandre Ayton earlier in the season, noting the comments added an unexpected storyline to the first‑round matchup between the Lakers and Rockets. The focus remains on the front‑court battle between the two big men.
key_points: Ayton’s comments referenced Capela’s defensive style and rebounding.
Capela said he was unaware of the remarks until recently.
The exchange highlights the personal angles within the broader series.

articles.title: Capela Responds to Ayton’s Comments
articles.original_title: Clint Capela finally responded to Deandre Ayton's comments - Lakers Nation
articles.summary: Clint Capela finally addressed Deandre Ayton’s earlier comments, offering a measured response that emphasized professionalism and respect. He downplayed any potential feud, focusing on the upcoming games.
key_points: Capela acknowledged Ayton’s remarks but called them “all part of the game.”
He stressed the importance of staying focused on team performance.
No escalation is expected; both players remain competitive on the court.

articles.title: Lakers Anticipate Austin Reaves’ Playoff Return as Luka Doncic Remains a Threat
articles.original_title: Lakers excited about Austin Reaves return for playoffs but Luka Doncic still looms large - MARCA

key_points: Reaves is cleared to play after a brief injury layoff.
His return adds depth to the Lakers’ backcourt alongside LeBron and Davis.
Doncic’s performance will be a key factor in the Lakers’ playoff outlook.

articles.title: Should Rockets Start Finney‑Smith Over Tate vs. Lakers?

articles.summary: Sports Illustrated debates whether Houston should give Dorian Finney‑Smith more minutes instead of Jae’Sean Tate in the upcoming game against Los Angeles. The article weighs defensive versatility against offensive consistency.
key_points: Finney‑Smith offers stronger perimeter defense against the Lakers’ shooters.
Tate provides more reliable scoring and floor spacing.
Coaches must decide which skill set better matches the Lakers’ offensive threats.

articles.title: Rockets Star Blames Kevin Durant After Lakers Double‑Team Woes
articles.original_title: Houston Rockets star challenges Kevin Durant after Game 2 disaster vs. LA Lakers’ double teams - Yahoo Sports UK
articles.summary: After a loss in Game 2 of the first‑round series, a Houston Rockets player publicly criticized Kevin Durant, saying his play contributed to the team's struggles against the Los Angeles Lakers' aggressive double‑team defense. The comment highlights tension within the Rockets' roster as they try to adjust to the Lakers' schemes.
key_points: The Rockets fell behind in Game 2 after the Lakers repeatedly doubled Kevin Durant.
A Rockets star (unnamed in the excerpt) blamed Durant for the offensive breakdown.
The criticism underscores internal frustration as Houston seeks to rebound in the series.

articles.title: Luke Kennard Praises JJ Redick Partnership
articles.original_title: Lakers News: Luke Kennard Explains Why JJ Redick Partnership Has Been So Great
articles.summary: Luke Kennard explained why his fit with head coach JJ Redick has been successful since joining the Lakers at the trade deadline. Kennard notes that both he and Redick share a knock‑down three‑point skill set, while Kennard has also shown growth as an on‑ball defender in the rotation.
key_points: Kennard arrived at the trade deadline and quickly became a key rotation piece.
Both Kennard and Redick are elite three‑point shooters, creating strategic synergy.
Kennard has expanded his defensive responsibilities, adding value beyond shooting.

articles.title: JJ Redick Disappointed Luka Dončić Missed MVP Finalist List
articles.original_title: JJ Redick was disappointed that Luka Dončić was not an MVP finalist
articles.summary: JJ Redick expressed disappointment that Luka Dončić was not named an MVP finalist despite posting a league‑leading 33.5 points per game and earning Western Conference Player of the Month honors twice this season. Redick highlighted Dončić's Hall‑of‑Fame‑level performance as deserving of greater recognition.
key_points: Dončić averaged 33.5 PPG, the highest in the NBA for the season.
He was named Western Conference Player of the Month in January and March.
Redick felt the MVP voting overlooked Dončić's dominant season.

articles.title: Ayton Says LeBron and Redick Keep Lakers Focused Against Rockets
articles.original_title: Deandre Ayton: LeBron James & JJ Reddick Not Letting Lakers Get Comfortable Against Rockets
articles.summary: Deandre Ayton credited LeBron James and head coach JJ Redick for preventing the Los Angeles Lakers from getting complacent while leading the Houston Rockets 2‑0 in the first‑round series. Despite missing key players like Luka Dončić and Austin Reaves, the Lakers have relied on contributions from the entire roster to protect their home court.
key_points: Lakers hold a 2‑0 series lead over the Rockets despite injuries and roster gaps.
Ayton highlighted leadership from LeBron James and strategic guidance from JJ Redick.
Teamwide effort has been essential in maintaining home‑court advantage.

Key moments included clutch performances from Trae Young and a strong third‑quarter surge by the Raptors.
key_points: Hawks lead series 2-1 after winning Game 3 on the road.
Raptors erased a deficit to force a competitive Game 4.
Trae Young’s late‑game scoring and defensive stops were decisive.

articles.original_title: West playoff takeaways: Wolves take 2-1 lead on Nuggets

key_points: Timberwolves win Game 3 to go up 2-1 in the series.
Effective three‑point shooting and rebounding gave Minnesota the edge.
Nikola Jokić’s limited impact highlighted Denver’s struggles.

key_points: Knicks’ odds lengthen to 30‑1 after losing momentum in their series.

Odds movement driven by playoff results rather than offseason speculation.

key_points: Blazers exploring a trade package for Giannis Antetokounmpo.

key_points: Kennard's shooting percentages rose significantly in the postseason.

Improved shot selection led to higher efficiency.
Team spacing and ball movement created more open looks for Kennard.

key_points: Dončić is still out with a lower‑body injury; no definitive return date.

Medical staff projects a gradual return, potentially after the next two games.
